HOLZER SENIOR CARE CENTER

CCN: 365998 · BIDWELL, OH 45614 · Gallia County

Overview

Address
380 COLONIAL DRIVE, BIDWELL, OH 45614
Phone
7404465001
Certified beds
60
Avg daily residents
40 (67% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1995-04-10
Setting
Rural
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.15 3.32 3.66 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.71 0.58 0.83 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.67 0.74 0.78 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.77 2.00 2.05 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.38 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.97
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.59
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.45
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
30.6%
annual
RN turnover
22.2%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.18
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.85
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-08 14 14 0 96 1 96
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-14 6 6 0 28 1 28
Total weighted health score
79.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (31)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

28 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0646 Notify the appropriate authorities when residents with MD or ID services has a significant change in condition. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. E 2025-05-08 2025-06-02
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-10
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. E 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. G 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2021-10-18 2021-11-12
